qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal via unsanitized manifest.name in MCPB upload allows arbitrary file relocation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable code at src/controllers/mcpbController.ts:107 where manifest.name from an uploaded MCPB archive is concatenated into a file path via path.join without sanitization, enabling path traversal via ../ sequences. Same CWE-22, same sink (fs.renameSync, fs.mkdirSync with recursive:true), same missing validation as described in the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Registration endpoint allows any authenticated user to create admin accounts without authorization check
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability: missing authorization check on POST /api/auth/register (authController.ts) allowing admin account creation. The advisory describes path traversal via manifest.name in mcpbController.ts, not a privilege escalation via registration.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Settings export endpoint exposes all credentials to any authenticated user without admin check
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability: GET /api/mcp-settings/export (configController.ts) exposes credentials without admin check. The advisory describes path traversal via manifest.name in mcpbController.ts, not an information disclosure via settings export.

selected_finding.title: Path traversal via unsanitized manifest.name in MCPB upload allows arbitrary file relocation

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the exact same vulnerable code described in the advisory: src/controllers/mcpbController.ts:107 where manifest.name from an uploaded MCPB archive is used unsanitized in path.join to construct finalExtractDir, then passed to fs.mkdirSync and fs.renameSync. The PoC was executed successfully in a Docker container running the vulnerable version (v0.12.12) of MCPHub. A crafted .mcpb file with manifest.name containing ../../../../../tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal caused extracted files to be relocated to /target-repo/tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal, which is outside the intended upload directory data/uploads/mcpb/. The proof string EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF was confirmed in the relocated files.
evidence: Server log confirms: 'MCPB server extracted to: /target-repo/tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal' - outside intended upload dir
Upload API response confirms success with extractDir outside data/uploads/mcpb/
Directory at /target-repo/tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal contains extracted files: server/index.js, README.md, manifest.json
Proof string EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF found in /target-repo/tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal/server/index.js and README.md
realpath verification confirms traversal target is outside realpath of intended upload directory
Same vulnerable code location as advisory: mcpbController.ts:107 (path.join with unsanitized manifest.name), line 112 (mkdirSync recursive), line 116 (renameSync)

poc_executions.observed_effect: Uploaded a crafted .mcpb file with manifest.name=../../../../../tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal. The server extracted files to /target-repo/tmp/evohunt-poc-traversal (outside the intended upload directory /target-repo/data/uploads/mcpb). fs.mkdirSync created the traversal target directory, fs.renameSync moved extracted content (server/index.js, README.md, manifest.json) containing EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF proof string to the traversal target.
